summary: Backend-only regression + new P2 testing for Droplet Manager v2 after server.py was split into routers/* modules and email verification + password reset + TTL indexes were added. All 23 prior regression tests PASS; all 16 new tests for email verification, password reset, TTL indexes and the lockout-with-native-datetime regression PASS. Real Resend API confirmed sending emails (Resend message IDs returned in backend logs).

minor.issue: Resend free-tier limit is 2 requests/sec; under bursty load (e.g. test suite) some sends fail with 'Too many requests'. mailer.py correctly swallows the exception and the endpoint returns {ok:true, sent:false}, so requests do NOT 5xx — but verification emails will silently not arrive. Consider adding a tiny retry-with-backoff inside mailer.send_email (or a background queue) so a transient Resend rate-limit error is retried rather than dropped.

Endpoint is unauthenticated and unrate-limited (only login has the brute-force lockout). Anyone can spam /forgot-password for known emails which would burn through Resend quota and cause repeated emails to real users. Recommend reusing the lockout/rate-limit machinery (e.g. cap forgot-password requests per email to 3/hour).

Both rely on TTL on `expires_at`. MongoDB TTL monitor only sweeps every 60s, so a verify-email/reset-password attempt within ~1min of expiry can still hit the row. Code handles this correctly (manual expires_at check + 400). Just noting that TTL is for cleanup, not authoritative expiry — current implementation is correct.

action_items: Consider adding rate-limiting to /api/auth/forgot-password (and optionally /api/auth/resend-verification) to prevent abuse and Resend quota burn.
Consider a small retry-with-backoff in mailer.send_email so Resend's 2 req/sec rate-limit errors don't silently drop verification emails.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py split is clean: routers/{auth_routes,tokens,do_proxy,windows,wizard}.py each own a domain; shared db.py + deps.py are good. server.py is now ~110 lines.
auth_routes.py is 291 lines — borderline; if more endpoints are added (2FA, sessions list, etc.) consider splitting into auth_email.py + auth_password.py + auth_oauth.py.
_public_user() is duplicated in auth_routes.py and likely needed in other routers eventually — move to deps.py to keep DRY.
`secrets.token_urlsafe(32)` used for both verification and reset — good (cryptographically strong, URL-safe).
/api/auth/reset-password correctly clears login-attempt rows after a successful reset — good UX so a locked-out user isn't still locked after resetting.
POST /api/auth/forgot-password always returns {ok:true} regardless of user existence — correctly avoids email enumeration.

TTL index on login_attempts uses expireAfterSeconds=900 (15 min) on `last_attempt`, and last_attempt is now a native datetime — verified the document stores BSON datetime so TTL will actually reap it.
TTL on email_verification_tokens.expires_at and password_reset_tokens.expires_at uses expireAfterSeconds=0 (Mongo expires the doc when expires_at <= now) — correct idiom.

Google OAuth path in /api/auth/session sets email_verified=True for new users AND for existing email-only users on next sign-in (auth_provider becomes 'both'). Verified by code-read; runtime test skipped per instructions.
/api/auth/refresh still works post-refactor — issues a fresh access_token cookie from the refresh_token cookie.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_email_verification_and_reset.py (NEW — 16 tests for verification, reset, TTL indexes, lockout regression)
test_credentials: admin@dropletmanager.app / AdminDroplet!42 (seeded). Test users created with mailinator addresses (test_<rand>@mailinator.com) — these DO trigger real Resend sends, but mailinator inboxes don't deliver to real people.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates its own ephemeral user (test_<uuid8>@mailinator.com) via /api/auth/register. Tests do not clean up after themselves — users + verification/reset tokens accumulate in the DB. Recommend adding a periodic cleanup of TEST_/test_*@mailinator.com users or use a teardown fixture if running suite repeatedly in long-lived environments.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files in /app/backend/tests/: (1) test_droplet_manager_v2.py — auth + DO + wizard regression (23 tests); (2) test_email_verification_and_reset.py — P2 features (16 tests). Both use pymongo (sync) for direct DB inspection. Run both: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Real Resend API key is in backend/.env — every register / resend-verification / forgot-password call sends a real email; use mailinator.com or similar throwaway addresses. To extract verification or reset tokens without an inbox, query mongo directly: `db.email_verification_tokens.find_one({email})` or `db.password_reset_tokens.find_one({user_id})`.
